analysis.reasoning: Core message: The author, after reviewing reports on a bleak anti-corruption history, argues that a new commission is mandated by international and national law (not a donor fad), that the EU has never blocked any investigation, and that there is no alternative to functioning national institutions. Assessment per narrative: EU1 (EU successful story): Not promoted; no celebration of EU’s historical success or learning from past as a whole. EU2 (EU united): Not addressed; no mention of unity or agreements among member states. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens): Not applicable; the tweet is about anti-corruption and institutional mandates, not EU benefits to EU citizens internally. EU4 (EU avant-garde): Not relevant; no pioneering regulation claim. EU5 (EU global champion of fair causes): Partially related thematically to rule of law, but the tweet does not portray EU leadership in defending rights; it only says the EU has never blocked investigations. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world): The claim that “#EU has never blocked any investigation” supports an image of the EU as a constructive, non-obstructive partner in anti-corruption and rule of law, contributing positively to governance beyond the EU. This aligns best with EU6. Final determination: EU6.
